symptoms.symptom_name: Continuous involuntary leakage of watery stool following a period of reduced bowel frequency

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Overflow faecal incontinence secondary to faecal impaction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The reduced-frequency-then-leakage sequence is the hallmark of overflow around an impaction.
True infective diarrhoea is not usually preceded by days of constipation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding reduced bowel frequency with abdominal bloating over ~72 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive constipation with bloating precedes the overflow phase of impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular opioid analgesia without any co-prescribed aperient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Opioids reliably slow colonic transit; absent aperient prophylaxis is a direct mechanism for impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Red, raw, painful perianal skin with redness spreading toward the buttocks (caregiver-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged contact with liquid stool produces moisture-associated dermatitis; spreading redness flags possible secondary cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perianal excoriation extending to the gluteal cleft (clinician-staged)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal inspection and staging of the extent of breakdown require an in-person examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable rectal faecal impaction with overflow liquid stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Overflow faecal incontinence secondary to faecal impaction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A digital rectal examination demonstrating an impacted mass with liquid overflow confirms the diagnosis — a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Decreased anal sphincter tone, reduced voluntary squeeze pressure, altered perianal sensation, loss of anocutaneous reflex

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuromuscular sphincter findings on DRE explain the incontinence and cannot be assessed via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdomen mildly distended, non-tender, with active bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension is consistent with loaded bowel; non-tender abdomen with active sounds argues against acute obstruction or peritonitis at this moment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anal sphincter defect on endoanal ultrasound

investigation_details.expected_result: External or internal anal sphincter defect that would help confirm the incontinence mechanism
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sphincter integrity remains uncharacterised; arranged non-urgently via the GP, not needed acutely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Characterises the sphincter but is a later outpatient investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile (37.3), no rigors, no new confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors/confusion lowers current sepsis probability, but frail elders under-report so the safety-net remains.
Afebrile with no systemic inflammatory signs argues against an infective cause of the leakage.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visible blood in stool, no reported weight loss, no vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding/weight loss reduces but does not exclude malignancy — PR exam still required.
No haematochezia and no prior inflammatory history make IBD unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedbound with cognitive and mobility impairment (cannot reach toilet in time)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immobility and cognitive impairment remove behavioural continence safeguards and predispose to impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake over the last two days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced intake plus overflow fluid loss risks dehydration and AKI in a frail elder.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Overflow faecal incontinence secondary to faecal impaction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reduced frequency, then overflow leakage, on an opioid without aperient, with a palpable rectal impaction — together strongly specific for the overflow mechanism.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms impaction and sphincter tone and guides disimpaction — the pivotal hands-on step the AI cannot perform remotely.
Assess hydration and renal function given reduced intake and fluid loss.
Outpatient characterisation of sphincter defects; not required acutely.
Overflow leakage is not infective diarrhoea; routine stool cultures would anchor on the wrong diagnosis and delay disimpaction.
